summary: Merged PR #3283 (commit e0225babd) implements the core managed-runtime checkpoint capture lane, but the merged PR's verification handoff and current repository inspection show acceptance-critical gaps in partial-artifact reconciliation/concurrent idempotency evidence, complete bounded observability, the requested test matrix, and the hermetic real-boundary capture journey. New code and tests are still required.

requirements.description: Register agent_runtime.capture_workspace_checkpoint on the agent-runtime queue and nowhere else.
Use ManagedWorkspaceLocator rather than arbitrary absolute workspace paths for new capture requests.
Resolve through the managed run store and verify runtime, agent-run, workflow, step, and execution correlation.
Never pass a managed workspace to TemporalSandboxActivities for capture.
Capture tracked and untracked files, binary files, executable bits, and safe symlink metadata in worktree_archive.
Exclude runtime credentials, provider homes, control sockets, caches, and out-of-repository state.
Produce deterministic archive and manifest evidence with stable digests and configured capture limits.
Keep large payloads in artifacts and workflow-boundary results compact.
Make capture idempotent across retries and concurrent duplicate invocations, including partial-write reconciliation and immutable-input conflicts.
Preserve a durable successful primary execution when managed capture fails.
Route the compact managed capture result into the canonical Step Execution checkpoint writer and persist a boundary checkpoint ref.
Keep checkpoint Resume ineligible for capture-only codex_cli support.
Keep existing sandbox and external-state checkpoint paths under their own owners and policies.
Preserve replayability of existing Temporal histories through an explicit patch/version cutover.
Emit the requested complete set of bounded capture metrics/events without high-cardinality labels.
Provide the full requested unit/security/idempotency test matrix and a hermetic journey using the real managed run store, locator resolution, archive builder, and artifact boundary.
